hero.title: 5 Days, Chennai Coast.
hero.titleAccent: City, Shore Temples and Pondicherry's French Quarter.
hero.intro: The Coromandel coast at its best. Chennai's culture, the ancient shore temples of Mahabalipuram, and a taste of French India in Pondicherry. Five days along one of South India's most rewarding coastlines.

overview.heading: 5 Days Chennai & Mahabalipuram: The Full Picture
body: This 5 day coastal tour explores the best of the Coromandel coast south of Chennai. From the Tamil capital's temples and beaches, you travel down to Mahabalipuram, with its UNESCO-listed shore temples and rock carvings, and on to Pondicherry, where the French colonial quarter feels worlds away from the rest of India.

Arranged privately from Malaysia, the trip blends heritage, beach time and culture, with your own vehicle and driver guide. The relaxed coastal pace, a beach resort stay, and the contrast between ancient temples and French boulevards make it a varied, enjoyable journey.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: Mahabalipuram's Shore Temples
items.body: The UNESCO-listed Shore Temple, the Pancha Rathas and the giant rock relief of Arjuna's Penance, masterpieces of Pallava art set right by the sea.

items.title: Pondicherry's French Quarter
items.body: Pastel colonial villas, tree-lined boulevards, seaside promenades and French-influenced cafes make Pondicherry one of the most charming towns in South India.

items.title: Coastal Relaxation
items.body: A beach resort stay along the Coromandel coast offers time to unwind between the heritage stops, with the Bay of Bengal at your doorstep.

days.title: Arrive Chennai
days.description: Met at Chennai airport and transferred to your hotel. Evening at Marina Beach.

days.title: Chennai to Mahabalipuram
days.description: Drive down the coast to Mahabalipuram and its monuments.

days.title: Mahabalipuram to Pondicherry
days.description: Coastal drive to the French town of Pondicherry.

days.title: Pondicherry & Return
days.description: More of Pondicherry, then return towards Chennai.

days.title: Depart Chennai
days.description: Checkout and transfer to the airport for your onward flight.

hotelOptions.heading: Choose Your Comfort Level

whyBook.heading: The Coromandel Coast, at Your Pace
whyBook.body: Linking Chennai, Mahabalipuram and Pondicherry with a beach stay takes good routing. We sequence the heritage and relaxation so the trip flows. Dhesu has run South India tours since 1988.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. We guide you through India e-Visa documentation with every booking.

points.title: Private Tours, Daily Departures
points.body: No minimum group to wait for. Your trip runs on your dates, your pace, your own vehicle and guide.

points.title: A Real Team to Call
points.body: Reachable throughout your trip. Flight delayed, your guide waits. Plans change, we adapt on the ground.

points.title: Group & Custom Pricing
points.body: Travelling as a family or group lowers the per-person cost. Ask us for a tailored quote.

sections.heading: Chennai & Mahabalipuram Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
This 5 day coastal tour is ideal for travellers who want a mix of heritage, beach and culture in South India. The route takes in Chennai, the UNESCO monuments of Mahabalipuram, and the French charm of Pondicherry.

A private vehicle and driver guide, plus a beach resort stay, make it a relaxed and varied journey along the Coromandel coast.

For a deeper temple focus, see our 4 day Tirupati and Navagraha temple tours; for a wider loop, the 7 day Tamil Nadu tour.

sections.heading: Mahabalipuram and Pondicherry
sections.body:
Mahabalipuram (Mamallapuram) is a UNESCO World Heritage Site, famed for its 7th-century Pallava rock-cut temples and the iconic Shore Temple by the Bay of Bengal.

Pondicherry, a former French colony, offers a complete change of mood, with its pastel villas, boulevards, seaside promenade and Franco-Tamil cuisine. Together they make a fascinating coastal pairing.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Chennai International Airport (MAA). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: What is the best time for this coastal trip?
items.answer: November to February is the most comfortable, with cooler, drier weather. March to June is hot and humid. The northeast monsoon brings rain to the coast around October to December. Winter is ideal for the beaches and temples.

items.question: Is there a beach resort stay?
items.answer: Yes. The package includes a beach resort stay along the Coromandel coast, typically around Mahabalipuram, offering time to relax by the Bay of Bengal between the heritage stops.

items.question: What is special about Pondicherry?
items.answer: Pondicherry was a French colony and retains a distinct character: pastel colonial buildings, a seaside promenade, French-influenced cafes and the serene Sri Aurobindo Ashram. It feels quite different from the rest of South India and is a highlight of the trip.
